channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: UK Army Chief Acknowledges Harm Caused by BATUK in Kenya, No Clear Accountability Measures
item.description: In a visit to the British Army Training Unit Kenya (BATUK) in Nanyuki on March 19, 2026, General Sir Roly Walker, the Chief of the General Staff of the British Army, acknowledged the historical abuse,...

item.title: KwaZulu-Natal Communities Receive Title Deeds in Land Restitution Ceremony
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa is set to preside over a significant land restitution milestone today, officially handing over title deeds to communities in uMzimkhulu, KwaZulu-Natal. The ceremony, taking p...

item.title: LTO Debunks Rumors of 100% Increase in Vehicle Registration Fees
item.description: The Land Transportation Office (LTO) has officially dismissed circulating reports claiming a 100% increase in vehicle registration fees. The agency strongly refuted the misinformation, stressing that ...

item.title: Pete Buttigieg tours the country to build a Democratic coalition ahead of 2028 elections
item.description: The article describes how former Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg is spending the 2026 midterm cycle traveling across the United States to support Democratic candidates while quietly positionin...

item.title: Gold prices decline amid US troop deployment and Middle East tensions
item.description: Gold prices slipped by 1.8 percent on Friday after reports emerged that the United States would be deploying additional troops to the Middle East. The news strengthened the US dollar and pushed Treasu...

item.title: How Excess Possessions Can Take Over Our Lives
item.description: In this reflective opinion piece, Andrew Wood explores the modern challenge of overaccumulation of possessions and its impact on our well-being. Living in a modest Toronto townhouse, Wood finds his ho...

item.title: Impeachment counsel says VP Sara Duterte is duty-bound to attend House hearings but doubts she will appear

item.title: Andhra Pradesh Government Transfers Five IAS Officers in Minor Administrative Reshuffle
item.description: The Andhra Pradesh government has carried out a minor reshuffle by transferring five Indian Administrative Service (IAS) officers, as per an order issued on March 20, 2026. K. Kanna Babu, who was serv...

item.title: Dubai's Property Market Faces Decline Amidst Regional Conflict
item.description: Dubai's property market, once a symbol of luxury and growth, is showing early signs of decline. Data from analysts reveals a significant drop in real estate transactions, with volumes falling by 37% y...

item.title: Hunter in Upper Chitral Accidentally Shoots Young Ibex Due to Weapon Malfunction
item.description: A tragic mishap took place during a licensed trophy hunt in the Barp area of Upper Chitral, where a non-local hunter unintentionally killed a juvenile Himalayan ibex. The hunter, who had invested sign...

item.title: President and PM Stress Muslim Unity on Eid Amid Regional Challenges
item.description: On the occasion of Eidul Fitr 2026, both President Asif Ali Zardari and Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif highlighted the importance of unity and brotherhood among Muslims, especially during difficult tim...

item.title: Manitoba to Increase Child-Care Funding in Upcoming Provincial Budget
item.description: According to Manitoba’s finance minister, the province’s upcoming budget will feature a notable increase in funding for child-care programs. This plan aims to make early childhood education more affor...

item.title: Zanzibar registers 45 new investment projects worth $218 million in first 100 days of government term
item.description: Zanzibar has shown strong investor confidence, registering 45 new investment projects worth over 218 million US dollars (about 567.6bn/-) in the first 100 days of the current government’s second term....

item.title: Building Industry Warns Rising Fuel Costs Threaten New Home Construction
item.description: Rising fuel prices, influenced by the conflict in the Middle East, are placing significant pressure on Australia's building and construction sector, putting the government's target of 1.2 million new ...

item.title: Iran’s Proxy Networks and Their Limited Role in the Current Regional Conflict
item.description: Since the start of the ongoing war in the Middle East, Iran’s network of proxy groups has displayed a highly uneven level of activity, reflecting the broader instability in the region. Iran has histor...

item.title: Voluntary Return of Burundian Refugees from Tanzanian Camps
item.description: About 11,000 Burundian refugees wey bin dey Nduta and Nyarugusu refugee camps for Kigoma region, Tanzania, don start dey return go Burundi on their own choice. The refugees talk say dem dey grateful t...

item.title: Hugo Broos Reflects on Legacy as Bafana Bafana Qualifies for the World Cup
item.description: Bafana Bafana coach Hugo Broos has defended his legacy amidst criticism from sections of South African football. Despite facing scrutiny, Broos expressed confidence in the job he has done with the tea...

item.title: Philippines and United States Conduct Bilateral Maritime Cooperative Activity in Manila Bay and Off Bataan
